Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Im calculating the duration for each case spanning from single day intervals to multi day periods The slowest cases are 105 102 and 104 while 101 and 103 are the fastest and Im starting to analyze what factors might be driving these differences

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Looking at the transition times I can see that escalations create significant delays case 105 has a massive 28 hour wait between escalation and L2 investigation Cases that span overnight hours also show extended durations particularly when investigation to resolution happens across non working periods

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Theres also a pattern where assignment to investigation queue wait correlates with cases that started later in the day suggesting L1 backlog issues

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Case 105 stands out as having rework it went through investigation twice due to a triage misclassification which wasted effort before escalation became necessary

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The key improvements would be better triage routing to send complex cases directly to L2 establishing SLAs for escalation pickup adding evening coverage implementing WIP limits and creating a dedicated escalation queue

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Im also noticing that tickets arriving later in the morning batch cases 104 and 105 experienced longer triage and assignment waits pointing to congestion during peak arrival times

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Now Im calculating the precise total durations and working toward an average and median to summarize the overall performance

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The pattern points to a working hours boundary where resolutions consistently happen around 08 00 to 09 00 the next morning

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: I should recommend triage based routing to screen complexity upfront establish escalation SLAs ensure L2 coverage across shifts assign work before investigation to eliminate queue delays avoid starting investigations near end of day and implement WIP limits to measure waiting versus processing time separately
